new_classes.definition_en: Vodka, a spirit drink produced from ethyl alcohol of agricultural origin obtained from potatoes and/or cereals, rectified, filtered and/or redistilled, with an alcoholic strength by volume of less than 80 % vol, classified under CN subheadings 2208 60 11 (in containers of 2 litres or less) and 2208 60 99 (in containers of more than 2 litres) (Chapter 22, heading 2208).

new_classes.label_en: Vodka in Containers of 2 Litres or Less (CN 22086011)
new_classes.label_de: Wodka in Behältnissen mit einem Inhalt von 2 Litern oder weniger (KN 22086011)
new_classes.definition_en: Vodka presented in containers with a nominal volume of 2 litres or less, classified under CN code 2208 60 11.

coverage_explanation: The legal note for 22086011 (together with 22086099) references 'Wodka' (Vodka) and points to the HS explanatory notes for heading 2208, third paragraph, item 5. The key distinguishing criteria are: (1) the product is Vodka — captured by introducing the Vodka class as a subclass of Spirit; (2) the container volume ≤ 2 L discriminates 22086011 from 22086099 — captured via the maxContainerVolumeL restriction and the cnHeadingCode hasValue axiom. What cannot be fully captured without additional HS-note content: the precise raw-material basis (potatoes/cereals), rectification/filtration requirements, and any organoleptic specifications referenced in HS note paragraph 5. These criteria reduce the coverage score to ~0.72.
